ops.title: cc-orchestrate — daemonkit migration (version resolver only)
ops.body:
cc-orchestrate's daemonkit adoption is version resolution, nothing more. `buildVersion` (`orchestrate/app.go:35`) delegates to `daemonkit/version.Running(Version)`: a stamped release passes through untouched, and an unstamped dev build resolves to the `9999.<mtime-nanos>.0-dev` sentinel — byte-identical to the hand-rolled executable-mtime fallback it replaced, so nothing downstream sees a different string.

## Comparator compatibility

The daemon eviction that consumes these strings still compares with cc-interact's `version.Newer`, not daemonkit's. `TestCrossComparatorSentinelOrdering` (`orchestrate/app_test.go:75`) pins that daemonkit-shaped strings order identically under both comparators: a dev sentinel beats every release, sentinels order by nanosecond, and releases order by triple. Change either comparator and that test is the tripwire.

## Explicitly not adopted

daemonkit's trust, wire, takeover, drain, and KeepAlive policy are all unused here. The supervisor also stays local by design — it is the donor `supervise` was generalized from, and its persisted, recovery-resettable restart budget has no daemonkit home yet.

One number to know before adopting wire later: daemonkit's standalone framing defaults to a 1 MiB frame cap (`wire.DefaultMaxLine`), not the 64 MiB cc-orchestrate's current wiring assumes. Set `Framing.MaxLine` explicitly or large payloads fail with `ErrFrameTooLarge`.

Migration commit: f1f15ca.
ops.when: when touching cc-orchestrate version stamping or considering wider daemonkit adoption
